        return None
    effective_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"Make the OneCycle peak a bit more aggressive and switch to AdamW, which is often a better fit for flattened CIFAR-10 MLPs under a fixed-step budget. This keeps the script simple while testing whether the current gain can be extended with a slightly larger effective step size.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Optimizer hyperparameters ----------------------------------------------
OPTIMIZER = "adam"
LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3
WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4
MOMENTUM = 0.9
ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"# --- Optimizer hyperparameters ----------------------------------------------

ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"Restore the more effective OneCycle shape from the best run and give it a wider MLP, since the current small model is likely underfitting once the schedule is working. This keeps the change focused on capacity while preserving the fixed-step training dynamics that helped most so far.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Architecture hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
DEPTH = 2
BASE_CHANNELS = 12
CHANNEL_MULT = 2
USE_BATCHNORM = True
DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0
FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"# --- Architecture hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
DEPTH = 3
BASE_CHANNELS = 16
CHANNEL_MULT = 2
USE_BATCHNORM = True
DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0
FC_HIDDEN = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_function","function":"build_scheduler","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    effective_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=max(2, effective_steps),
        pct_start=0.1,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=100.0,
    )"}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["layout","summaries"],"rationale":"Undo the wider-model regression, then test a larger batch under the same best-performing optimizer family and budget-aware schedule. With fixed-step training, a bigger batch can expose more examples per update and often helps BatchNorm-equipped MLPs more than adding depth.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Architecture hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
DEPTH = 3

CHANNEL_MULT = 2
USE_BATCHNORM = True

FC_HIDDEN = 48","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Batch / data hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
BATCH_SIZE = 64
NUM_WORKERS = 0","new":"# --- Batch / data hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
BATCH_SIZE = 128
NUM_WORKERS = 0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_function","function":"build_scheduler","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    effective_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=max(2, effective_steps),
        pct_start=0.1,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=100.0,
    )"}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The larger batch was the last successful move, so test the same fixed-step advantage one step further by giving each update even more data. The model is still small enough that a 256 batch should be feasible on CPU and may improve BatchNorm stability as well.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The last gain came from giving each of the 256 fixed steps more data, so continue that trend with one more batch-size increase. The network is small enough that a 512 batch should still be feasible and may further improve BatchNorm stability and sample coverage per update.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The batch-size scaling has been the strongest effect so far, and the model is still small enough to tolerate another increase. Pushing more examples through each of the fixed steps is the simplest way to keep exploiting that trend.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The large-batch regime is working, so the next clean knob is the peak learning rate in the OneCycle schedule. A slightly higher max LR is the simplest way to test whether the current run is still under-driving the optimizer at batch 1024.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The large-batch setup is now stable, so the next low-risk axis is to ease off regularization a bit. Lowering weight decay should let the small MLP use the extra training signal from the huge batches more fully.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The regularization tweak was neutral, so go back to the signal that has consistently helped: giving each fixed step more data. The MLP is still small enough that another batch-size increase is the cleanest remaining high-leverage move.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"Keep the large-batch setup and probe a slightly higher peak learning rate. The current run is still improving, so a small upward LR adjustment is the simplest remaining lever for the fixed-step OneCycle regime.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"indexing","declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The run is now in a large-batch regime where the optimizer hyperparameters matter more than the regularization knobs. Raising AdamW's beta2 should smooth the update statistics a bit more and may recover a little extra stability at this scale.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The beta2 increase regressed a bit, so keep the better large-batch optimizer state and nudge the peak learning rate higher instead. This is the cleanest remaining knob for the current OneCycle setup.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The larger peak LR still produced a small gain, so continue the same sweep one notch higher. The current large-batch OneCycle regime is the clearest remaining knob, and this keeps the change minimal.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The peak LR has been trending upward with consistent, if shrinking, gains. Keep the same large-batch schedule and probe one more step higher to see if the monotonic improvement continues.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The peak LR has still been moving in the right direction, just with smaller gains. One more step higher is the cleanest final topk probe before the run ends.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The last step shows the peak LR is still near the optimum, but 8e-3 overshot slightly. Probe an intermediate value between the 7e-3 best point and the 8e-3 regression instead of moving farther out.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The best point sits very close to the current learning-rate neighborhood, so the only sensible final probe is a small interpolation around it rather than a larger jump. This keeps the same large-batch schedule and just refines the peak LR near the apparent optimum.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7.25e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}
